meta_data.format_description: tensorQTL cis-nominal mode with interaction term output format

header_description.start_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype start position (e.g., TSS)
header_description.end_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype end position (only present if different from start position)
header_description.af: In-sample ALT allele frequency of the variant
header_description.ma_samples: Number of samples carrying at least one minor allele
header_description.pval_g: Nominal p-value of the genotype term
header_description.pval_i: Nominal p-value of the interaction variable
header_description.pval_gi: Nominal p-value of the interaction term
header_description.tests_emt: Effective number of independent variants (M_eff) estimated by eigenMT

meta_data.format_description: Variant table for .pgen; VCF-style headers allowed. Last header line starts with '#CHROM'. Without headers, columns follow .bim order (CHROM, ID, CM, POS, ALT, REF or 5-column without CM).

meta_data.format_comment: BGENIE output is space-separated and gzip compressed. Phenotype-specific columns follow the pattern {pheno_name}_beta, {pheno_name}_se, {pheno_name}_t, and optionally {pheno_name}_p (if --pvals flag is used). The beta coefficient refers to the effect of having an extra copy of a_1 (the second allele).

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Union of common association columns across .assoc, .assoc.fisher, .assoc.linear, .assoc.logistic, and .assoc.dosage; see per-extension specs in formats/plink_*.json.

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Multi-covariate logistic regression from --logistic. BETA column appears with '--logistic beta'; otherwise OR. With --ci 0.xy, SE, L_xy, U_xy are inserted before STAT (SE is on log-odds scale).

header_description.NMISS: Observations with nonmissing genotype, phenotype, and covariates
header_description.L95: Lower end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)

meta_data.format_description: tensorQTL cis-nominal mode output format

header_description.start_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype start position (e.g., TSS)
header_description.end_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype end position (only present if different from start position)
header_description.af: In-sample ALT allele frequency of the variant
header_description.ma_samples: Number of samples carrying at least one minor allele
header_description.pval_nominal: Nominal p-value of the association between the phenotype and variant
header_description.slope_se: Standard error of the regression slope

meta_data.format_citation: Lyon, M.S., Andrews, S.J., Elsworth, B. et al. The variant call format provides efficient and robust storage of GWAS summary statistics. Genome Biol 22, 32 (2021). https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-020-02248-0
meta_data.format_fixed_header:
##fileformat=VCFv4.2
##FILTER=<ID=PASS,Description="All filters passed">
##INFO=<ID=AF,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Allele Frequency">
##FORMAT=<ID=ES,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Effect size estimate relative to the alternative allele">
##FORMAT=<ID=SE,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Standard error of effect size estimate">
##FORMAT=<ID=LP,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="-log10 p-value for effect estimate">
##FORMAT=<ID=AF,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Alternate allele frequency in the association study">
##FORMAT=<ID=SS,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Sample size used to estimate genetic effect">
##FORMAT=<ID=EZ,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Z-score provided if it was used to derive the EFFECT and SE fields">
##FORMAT=<ID=SI,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Accuracy score of summary data imputation">
##FORMAT=<ID=NC,Number=A,Type=Float,Description="Number of cases used to estimate genetic effect">
##FORMAT=<ID=ID,Number=1,Type=String,Description="Study variant identifier">
##META=<ID=TotalVariants,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of variants in input">
##META=<ID=VariantsNotRead,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Number of variants that could not be read">
##META=<ID=HarmonisedVariants,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of harmonised variants">
##META=<ID=VariantsNotHarmonised,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of variants that could not be harmonised">
##META=<ID=SwitchedAlleles,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of variants strand switched">
##META=<ID=TotalControls,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of controls in the association study">
##META=<ID=TotalCases,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Total number of cases in the association study">
##META=<ID=StudyType,Number=1,Type=String,Description="Type of GWAS study [Continuous or CaseControl]">

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Case/control basic allelic test from --assoc (not --linear/--logistic). Default columns; 'counts' replaces F_A/F_U with C_A/C_U; --ci 0.xy appends SE, L_xy, U_xy after OR.

header_description.CHISQ: Allelic test chi-square (omitted with fisher / fisher-midp)
header_description.SE: Standard error of odds ratio estimate (--ci)
header_description.L95: Lower end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)

meta_data.format_description: Logistic or Firth logistic from --glm (case/control). LOG(OR)_SE is SE on log-odds scale; Z_STAT is Wald Z. Column set varies with cols= and case/control count columns.

header_description.L95: Lower symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Multi-covariate linear regression from --linear. With --ci 0.xy, SE, L_xy, U_xy are inserted before STAT. Column count varies with 'genotypic', 'hethom', and --tests.

header_description.NMISS: Observations with nonmissing genotype, phenotype, and covariates
header_description.L95: Lower end of symmetric approx. CI for beta (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper end of symmetric approx. CI for beta (--ci)

meta_data.format_description: Same column layout as .glm.logistic when Firth logistic regression is used (extension .glm.firth or .glm.logistic.hybrid).

header_description.FIRTH?: Whether Firth regression was used ('firth-fallback' only)

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_2 assumes A2=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_2_alt_eaf`
Note: auto_neaf assumes A1=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=NEAF — same as `auto_1_alt_neaf`

meta_data.format_citation: Gagliano Taliun, S.A., VandeHaar, P. et al. Exploring and visualizing large-scale genetic associations by using PheWeb. Nat Genet 52, 550–552 (2020).
format_notes: Variants must be sorted by chromosome and position, with chromosomes in order [1-22,X,Y,MT]
Reference allele must match the reference genome specified in config.py (hg_build_number 19 or 38)
If pval is log10 (e.g., REGENIE output), set pval_is_neglog10 = True in config.py
Custom column names can be mapped using field_aliases in config.py

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_1_ref_neaf assumes A1=EA; REF=EA; Frq=NEAF — same as `auto_ref_neaf`

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Case/control allelic test with Fisher (or Fisher mid-p); same layout as .assoc but CHISQ is not written.

header_description.SE: Standard error of odds ratio estimate (--ci)
header_description.L95: Lower end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper end of symmetric approx. CI for OR (--ci)

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_0 assumes A0=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_0_alt_eaf`
Note: auto_0_ref_neaf assumes A0=EA; REF=EA; Frq=NEAF — no shorter format_name
Note: auto_ref assumes A1=EA; REF=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_1_ref` / `auto_1_ref_eaf`

meta_data.format_citation: Peyrot, W. J., & Price, A. L. (2021). Identifying loci with different allele frequencies among cases of eight psychiatric disorders using CC-GWAS. Nature genetics, 53(4), 445-454.

meta_data.format_description: Linear regression from --glm (quantitative phenotype). Header line starts with '#'. Column set can be reduced with cols=; optional NEG_LOG10_P and joint F_STAT appear with modifiers.

header_description.L95: Lower symmetric approx. CI for beta (--ci)
header_description.U95: Upper symmetric approx. CI for beta (--ci)

meta_data.format_citation: Marchini, J., Howie, B., Myers, S., McVean, G., & Donnelly, P. (2007). A new multipoint method for genome-wide association studies via imputation of genotypes. Nature Genetics, 39(7), 906-913.
meta_data.software_license: SNPTEST is available free to use for academic use only.

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Extended variant map with .bed: CHR, SNP, CM, BP, A1 (first/clear-bit allele, usually minor), A2 (second/set-bit allele, usually major). PLINK 2 .bim lists ALT before REF but encodes the same roles as 1.x A1/A2.

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_2_neaf assumes A2=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=NEAF — same as `auto_2_alt_neaf`
Note: auto_2_ref_neaf assumes A2=EA; REF=EA; Frq=NEAF — no shorter format_name

meta_data.format_description: tensorQTL cis-independent mode output format

header_description.beta_shape1: Parameter of the fitted Beta distribution
header_description.beta_shape2: Parameter of the fitted Beta distribution
header_description.true_df: Degrees of freedom used to compute p-values
header_description.start_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype start position (e.g., TSS)
header_description.end_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype end position (only present if different from start position)
header_description.ma_samples: Number of samples carrying at least one minor allele
header_description.af: In-sample ALT allele frequency of the variant
header_description.pval_nominal: Nominal p-value of the association between the phenotype and variant
header_description.slope_se: Standard error of the regression slope
header_description.rank: Rank of the variant for the phenotype

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_ref_neaf assumes A1=EA; REF=EA; Frq=NEAF — same as `auto_1_ref_neaf`
Note: auto_0_neaf assumes A0=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=NEAF — same as `auto_0_alt_neaf`

meta_data.format_header_line2_description: Second header line specifies column types: '0' for ID/covariates, 'D' for discrete/categorical, 'B' for binary, 'P' for continuous phenotype
meta_data.format_description: Sample information file (.sample) accompanying .gen, .bgen genotype dosage files, or .haps phased reference panels. Loaded with --data/--sample, and produced by --export. The file is space-delimited with two header lines followed by one line per sample.

header_description.sex: Sex code ('1' = male, '2' = female, '0' = unknown; header line 2 value: D)

meta_data.format_description: tensorQTL cis mode output format with permutations

header_description.beta_shape1: Parameter of the fitted Beta distribution
header_description.beta_shape2: Parameter of the fitted Beta distribution
header_description.true_df: Degrees of freedom used to compute p-values
header_description.start_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype start position (e.g., TSS)
header_description.end_distance: Distance between the variant and phenotype end position (only present if different from start position)
header_description.ma_samples: Number of samples carrying at least one minor allele
header_description.af: In-sample ALT allele frequency of the variant
header_description.pval_nominal: Nominal p-value of the association between the phenotype and variant
header_description.slope_se: Standard error of the regression slope
header_description.pval_nominal_threshold: Nominal p-value threshold for significant associations with the phenotype

meta_data.format_description: tensorQTL trans mode output format

header_description.pval: Nominal p-value of the association between the phenotype and variant
header_description.b_se: Standard error of the regression slope
header_description.r2: Squared residual genotype-phenotype correlation (only generated if map_trans(..., return_r2=True))
header_description.af: In-sample ALT allele frequency of the variant

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_0_ref assumes A0=EA; REF=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_0_ref_eaf`

meta_data.software_license: BOLT-LMM is free software under the GNU General Public License v3.0 (GPLv3).
meta_data.format_citation: Loh, P. R., Tucker, G., Bulik-Sullivan, B. K., Vilhjalmsson, B. J., Finucane, H. K., Salem, R. M., ... & Price, A. L. (2015). Efficient Bayesian mixed-model analysis increases association power in large cohorts. Nature genetics, 47(3), 284-290.

meta_data.format_description: Sample table for .pgen. Last header line starts with '#FID' or '#IID'; FID may be omitted (then assumed '0'). Columns after SEX are phenotype/covariate names.

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_2_ref assumes A2=EA; REF=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_2_ref_eaf`

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Per-sample row for .bed: FID, IID, father IID, mother IID, sex, phenotype. Quantitative phenotype if numeric values other than {-9,0,1,2} appear.

meta_data.format_citation: Zhou, X., & Stephens, M. (2012). Genome-wide efficient mixed-model analysis for association studies. Nature Genetics, 44(7), 821-824.
meta_data.format_comment: GEMMA univariate linear mixed model (LMM) output format. Columns: chr, rs, ps, n_miss, allele1, allele0, af, beta, se, l_remle, p_wald. The output is tab-separated. allele1 is the effect allele, allele0 is the non-effect allele, af is the allele frequency of allele1, and l_remle is the log-likelihood ratio test statistic.

meta_data.format_description: Shared raw→canonical aliases for all auto* presets (no allele or ambiguous-frequency assumptions). Merged by scripts/sync_auto_from_raw.py; not sufficient alone for summary-stat allele harmonization.
Union of columns across .glm.linear, .glm.logistic, and .glm.firth; full column sets are in formats/plink2_*.json.

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto_1_ref assumes A1=EA; REF=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_ref` / `auto_1_ref_eaf`

meta_data.format_citation: Purcell, S., Neale, B., Todd-Brown, K., Thomas, L., Ferreira, M. A., Bender, D., ... & Sham, P. C. (2007). PLINK: a tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American journal of human genetics, 81(3), 559-575.
meta_data.format_description: Dosage association from --dosage. CHR/BP require --map. 'case-control-freqs' replaces FRQ with FRQ_A and FRQ_U.

header_description.SE: Standard error of effect (not OR SE)

meta_data.format_assumption: Note: auto assumes A1=EA; Alt=EA; Frq=EAF — same as `auto_1_alt_eaf`
